# Evergreen Notes

Evergreen notes are written and organized to evolve, contribute, and accumulate over time, across projects. This is in contrast to notes that are made as a temporary scratch pad while reading.

## Properties

Evergreen notes should be atomic — each note covers one concept.
Evergreen notes should be concept-oriented, not author or source-oriented.
Evergreen notes should be densely linked to other notes.

## Relationship to Zettelkasten

The evergreen note philosophy is closely related to Zettelkasten. Both emphasize atomicity and linking. Evergreen notes tend to be expressed in more complete prose, developed iteratively over time.
